既存の Phase5/6 のレポート命名規約と design_context の位置づけが未確定

completion_checks.name: initial_real_project_trial
confirmed_facts: session-40 executed the builder-to-reviewer flow on a real project input
the reviewer returned reject with five issues
all five issues were treated as valid and actionable
completion_checks.interpretation: The generation layer was operationally usable enough to expose concrete builder weaknesses through a real trial.

completion_checks.name: builder_prompt_strengthening
confirmed_facts: session-41 strengthened the builder prompt in three areas
the three areas were repository path convention, out_of_scope to forbidden mapping, and acceptance self-validation
the improvement design was recorded as Phase10 builder improvement design
completion_checks.interpretation: The main structural weaknesses found in session-40 were translated into explicit builder rules.

completion_checks.name: prompt_content_cleanup
confirmed_facts: session-41-fix removed non-prompt content from the builder template
the builder prompt was restored to prompt-only content
this prevented session-42 from being distorted by prompt contamination
completion_checks.interpretation: The builder revalidation used a clean prompt source instead of a mixed prompt-plus-notes artifact.

completion_checks.name: revalidation_cycle_execution
confirmed_facts: session-42 reran the builder-to-reviewer flow with the improved builder prompt
the main three weaknesses from session-40 were resolved
the session outcome moved from reject to conditional_pass
completion_checks.interpretation: The builder improvement materially improved output quality and reduced the number of blocking issues.

completion_checks.name: remaining_issue_resolution
confirmed_facts: session-42a corrected the objective wording to include review-ready output
session-42a converted acceptance_criteria into an object array with id, description, and test_function
session-42a created the session-42 trial report with remaining issue tracking
completion_checks.interpretation: The residual OS-convention misalignments from session-42 were resolved without expanding scope.

evidence_summary.trial_outcome: Phase10 ran one real-project builder-to-reviewer trial and exposed five concrete weaknesses in the initial builder output.
evidence_summary.improvement_outcome: Phase10 converted those weaknesses into explicit builder rules and cleaned the prompt source before revalidation.
evidence_summary.revalidation_outcome: The revalidation cycle improved the result from reject to conditional pass, and the remaining two structural issues were then corrected in session-42a.
evidence_summary.overall_assessment: Phase10 successfully demonstrated that the generation layer can be improved through a closed feedback loop and can reach an OS-consistent output after one refinement cycle.

risks: The validated trial subject was self-referential and may not expose all issue patterns that appear in unrelated external projects.
Reviewer strictness may still need tuning if future projects introduce more complex acceptance structures.
The builder-to-reviewer flow is still manual and not integrated into runtime execution.

current_position.summary: Phase10 successfully demonstrated that the generation layer can be improved through a closed feedback loop and can reach an OS-consistent output after one refinement cycle.

next_actions.action: Start Phase11 using the Phase10 trial report and improved builder prompt as the baseline generation layer.

final_judgement.reason: Phase10 achieved its purpose by executing a real builder-to-reviewer trial, identifying concrete builder weaknesses, applying targeted prompt improvements, and resolving the remaining structural issues through session-42a.
